summary: The source is the abstract of the seminal 1997 paper introducing Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM). It outlines how LSTM uses constant error carousels and multiplicative gate units to solve complex, long-time-lag tasks that previous recurrent neural networks could not handle.

principles.statement: To bridge long time lags in recurrent networks, you must enforce constant error flow.
principles.rationale: Enforcing constant error flow allows the network to bridge minimal time lags of over 1,000 discrete-time steps, solving complex tasks that previous recurrent algorithms could not.
principles.source_quote: LSTM can learn to bridge minimal time lags in excess of 1,000 discrete-time steps by enforcing constant error flow through constant error carousels within special units.

frameworks.name: Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM)
frameworks.when_to_apply: When dealing with complex sequence learning tasks that require bridging long time lags (in excess of 1,000 discrete-time steps).
steps: Enforce constant error flow through constant error carousels within special units.
Use multiplicative gate units to learn to open and close access to the constant error flow.
Apply a gradient-based learning algorithm that is local in space and time.
frameworks.source_quote: Multiplicative gate units learn to open and close access to the constant error flow. LSTM is local in space and time.

mental_models.name: Constant Error Carousels and Gates
mental_models.description: Viewing memory in a neural network as a protected 'carousel' where error flows constantly without degrading, controlled by 'gates' that learn when to open and close access to this flow.
mental_models.example: Using multiplicative gate units to protect the constant error flow from irrelevant inputs or noise, allowing the network to learn over long time lags.

signature_quotes.text: LSTM can learn to bridge minimal time lags in excess of 1,000 discrete-time steps by enforcing constant error flow through constant error carousels within special units.
signature_quotes.context: Explaining the core mechanism and capability of the LSTM architecture.

signature_quotes.text: LSTM also solves complex, artificial long-time-lag tasks that have never been solved by previous recurrent network algorithms.
signature_quotes.context: Highlighting the breakthrough nature of the LSTM algorithm compared to prior methods.

anti_patterns.description: Relying on traditional recurrent network algorithms (like back propagation through time, real-time recurrent learning, or Elman nets) for complex, long-time-lag tasks.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: Previous recurrent network algorithms are unable to solve complex, artificial long-time-lag tasks, whereas LSTM succeeds by enforcing constant error flow.
